codeReview.summary: Commit ef275044 satisfies the feature contract. It reconnects caller-facing id normalization after typed stored decode and foreign hydration by applying normalize_public_id_value only on hydrated public values, while leaving stored-row decode focused on RecordId-compatible shapes. The runtime diff also preserves arbitrary-table string-form RecordId compatibility through deserialize_record_id_or_compat_string and keeps ordinary colon-string payload protection in place by not restoring the old global recursive rewrite path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still lacks an explicit evidence-only review path for features whose implementation already landed before the worker session, which forced this feature worker to deviate from the normal test-first/code-change procedure even though the assigned task was only to backfill mission evidence.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, implementation commit ef275044, current runtime/test code, and shared-state files. The implementation restores plain caller-facing ids after hydration boundaries without reintroducing global string rewriting, and I found no blocking or non-blocking defects in this feature scope.
